stem: A pupil is starting work on a Python program in an Integrated Development Environment (IDE).
model_answer: An IDE bundles together tools that help write and debug code. Common features include: an editor with syntax highlighting and auto-indent so mistakes are visible at a glance; a built-in interpreter or 'run' button so the program can be tested without leaving the IDE; and a debugger with breakpoints, single-step, and a variable watch so the pupil can pause execution and inspect state. Most IDEs also offer error messages with line numbers and suggestions, and integrated documentation.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for at least three named IDE features with a one-line purpose for each.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe three features of an IDE that help a pupil write and debug a Python program.

mark_points.text: editor with syntax highlighting / auto-indent / line numbers
run/build button or integrated interpreter to execute without switching to a terminal
debugger (breakpoints / step-into / variable watch) — only one of these is enough
